empirical_object: paid and protected time away from work: vacation, sick leave, holidays, parental/family leave, personal/miscellaneous leave
why_not_more_final: some personal/miscellaneous leave subobjects require central aggregation and a few records remain review-flagged
next_production_step: keep as core scored domain; audit repeated leave aggregation on a larger sample

empirical_object: extra compensation for overtime, shift work, call-in/reporting, standby/on-call, and responsibility/specialty assignments
why_not_more_final: some premiums depend on tables, occupation-specific triggers, or unsupported fragments
next_production_step: keep as core scored domain; expand table checks for occupation-specific premiums

empirical_object: procedural protections around grievance, arbitration, discipline, just cause, progressive discipline, and investigation/appeal rights
why_not_more_final: some grievance/arbitration records remain central-review cases
next_production_step: keep as core scored domain; audit whether procedural scores behave consistently across sectors

empirical_object: layoff order, recall rights, benefit continuation, severance, and related displacement protections
why_not_more_final: severance and benefit-continuation terms sometimes require numeric normalization or context
next_production_step: keep as core scored domain; refine severance normalization and benefit-continuation rules

empirical_object: worker-facing contribution burden for active medical coverage, such as premium shares or fixed employee contributions
why_not_more_final: this measures contribution burden only, not full health plan design
next_production_step: use as narrow extension domain with coverage flags; do not label it full health generosity

empirical_object: medical plan design terms such as deductibles, copays, out-of-pocket maximums, dependent coverage, network design, prescription coverage, dental, vision, disability, and retiree health
why_not_more_final: many terms are in attachments or external plan documents, and plan generosity is multi-dimensional
next_production_step: separate contribution, plan design, dependent coverage, and retiree-health modules before scalar scoring

empirical_object: scheduled nominal wage increases converted to pilot-relative real wage growth where timing and percentage terms are comparable
why_not_more_final: excludes lump sums, wage/fringe packages, incomplete COLA formulas, and table-only changes
next_production_step: fix inflation date rule and treatment of lump sums, COLA, and wage/fringe allocations before production scoring

empirical_object: classification-specific base wage level with date, unit, worker group, and benchmark step preserved
why_not_more_final: only two documents are cleanly ready; three more need table/OCR review; raw wages require occupation, year, geography, hours, and step normalization
next_production_step: define benchmark occupation/step and table repair protocol before adding any wage-level score

empirical_object: pension, annuity, welfare, trust, and other external fund contributions or formula fragments stated in the CBA
why_not_more_final: CBA often states funding flow but worker-facing benefits depend on external plan documents
next_production_step: keep as total-compensation or funding proxy; do not fold into wage, health, or retirement generosity

empirical_object: regular schedules, schedule notice/change rules, hours frameworks, workload and staffing rules
why_not_more_final: many records define governance frameworks rather than directly comparable generosity quantities
next_production_step: develop separate scoreability rules for notice, predictability, employer discretion, and worker control

empirical_object: union business access, dues/security, representation time, and related institutional voice provisions
why_not_more_final: current records are partly institutional context and partly worker-facing protections; scoring object is not yet settled
next_production_step: separate worker-facing voice protections from institutional union-security context

empirical_object: recognition scope, bargaining-unit coverage, seniority systems, classification mobility, transfer and promotion frameworks
why_not_more_final: these define who is covered and how rights attach, but are not yet calibrated as generosity scores
next_production_step: treat as modifiers/context unless a specific scoreable worker-facing object is defined

empirical_object: PPE, unsafe-work refusal, assault/violence protections, and related safety provisions
why_not_more_final: many safety records look scoreable, but the domain has not yet been promoted into central domain profiles
next_production_step: consider as next candidate scored domain after auditing examples and score variation
